frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Standard PPC framework for integrating sensory evidence to a decision.

frameworks.name: transient/leaky integration
frameworks.one_line: Evidence influence decays over a short, task-matched timescale.

frameworks.name: urgency / collapsing-bound gain
frameworks.one_line: Gain on evidence rises with elapsed decision time.

frameworks.name: change-point detection
frameworks.one_line: Task frames the problem as detecting a transient change in a noisy stream.

core_question: How do PPC neurons adjust their dynamics to evaluate evidence over short, change-detection-relevant timescales?
key_assumptions: PPC encodes evidence used for the current decision
Behavioral timescale of evidence evaluation can be read from neural transients
Opposing evidence-tuned populations co-exist in PPC
manipulations: Strength of momentary evidence pulses in a noisy stream
Decision time elapsed within a trial
key_findings: PPC firing scaled with evidence strength and was choice-selective
Pulse responses were transient, decaying over the behavioral evaluation timescale
Evidence gain in PPC grew with decision time, consistent with rising urgency
interpretations: PPC implements a time-adaptive, short-timescale evidence evaluator rather than a pure long-timescale integrator
Urgency-like gain changes reshape evidence influence as a decision unfolds
